mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate exceeding 4.0% signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, causing equity markets to price in lower corporate revenues and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE crosses 4.0%, or signal loses predictive power (p>0.05) in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate earnings growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to produce positive 60-day returns in >50% of cases, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects improving supply-side capacity utilization, which reduces input cost pressures and boosts short-term equity momentum, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration fails to predict positive 5-day returns during tightening periods, or signal direction reverses consistently when inflation exceeds 3%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 0.4% forces the Federal Reserve to maintain restrictive policy, increasing recession probabilities and triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: CPI spikes above 0.4% are followed by positive 20-day returns in >65% of observations, or the threshold effect vanishes in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, supporting consumer income growth and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to predict positive returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects stronger aggregate economic activity, which translates into elevated corporate profit margins and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistically significant correlation with 60-day forward returns, or Rank IC remains below 0.01 across all tested periods.

mechanism_narrative: Two consecutive months of declining industrial production signal a supply-side contraction that raises recession odds, prompting risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Consecutive INDPRO declines are followed by positive 5-day returns in >60% of instances, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during periods of stable monetary policy.
